---

The Small Business Administration (or SBA) is a federal agency that helps to provide
loans to small businesses. However, the SBA does not issue the loans itself. SBA
partners with lenders to help increase small business access to loans.
What SBA Loans Can Be Used For
1. 	Buy land (including construction costs)
2. 	Buy or expand a currently operating business
3. 	Replace existing debt with new “SBA debt”
4. 	Buy machinery, equipment, F&F, supplies or materials
SBA Loan Requirements
1. 	Must be a for-profit business
2. 	Must do business in the United States
3. 	Business owner(s) must have reasonable invested equity
SBA Loan Programs Guide
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 6 --

4. 	The business is unable to get funds from other financing options
5. 	Must meet small business requirements
a. 	Click here to access the SBA Size Standards Tool to see if your business meets the
requirements
6. 	Must not be delinquent on any existing debt obligations to the U.S. government
Why SBA Loans
1. 	Better rates
a. 	Loan rates under 8%
b. 	See SBA 2022 loan rates here
2. 	Better terms
. 	SBA guarantees up to 85% of the loan
a. 	Rarely have to pay 10% down
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 6 --

b. 	Borrowing less than $350k likely requires no collateral
3. 	Help and support
. 	The SBA has a vested interest in your success — they may be able to support you
during the acquisition process
Pre-SBA Loan Checklist
Before you get started with applying for a loan, go through this Pre-SBA Loan Checklist:
• 	Have a business plan. Most lenders expect a business plan when you apply for
startup funding. If you need to create one, follow our free business plan guide.
• 	Have the amount needed and use of funds ready. Know how much capital you
need and how it will help your business. You can’t use an SBA-approved loan to flip a
house.
• 	Have credit history prepared. Lenders use credit scores to determine credit risk and
interest rates. SBA helps guarantee some loans that otherwise may not qualify.
• 	Have financial projections prepared. Show you understand your business' finances,
how the funds will be used, and how you'll pay back the loan.
• 	Be prepared to provide collateral. Many lenders require you to use another asset to
guarantee your loan. This can be a home, car, inventory, or other property you own.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 6 --

• 	Apply your industry experience. Industry experience isn't required, but it's helpful.
Firsthand knowledge about your industry can make your lender feel confident about
making a loan.
How To Get Started in 4 Easy Steps
Using the SBA Lender Match tool you’ll…
1. 	Describe your needs by answering a few questions about your business in as little as
five minutes.
2. 	Get matched. You’ll receive an email with contact information of lenders who express
interest in your loan.
3. 	Talk to matched lenders and compare rates, terms, fees, and more.
4. 	Apply for a loan with a lender.
Frequently Asked Questions
What happens after I apply?
According to the SBA, you should receive an email with contact information of interested
lenders two business days after you submit the form. From there, you’ll start talking to
lenders and completing applications. Some will reach out to you, and you’re welcome to
contact them as well. We provide a list of the most active lenders on the next page.
Am I guaranteed to get matched?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 6 --

No, using the Lender Match tool doesn't guarantee that you'll get matched or be offered a
loan. Lender Match isn't a loan application — it's a tool to help businesses find lenders in
their communities.
What should I ask a lender?
Ask lenders about interest rates, minimum credit score, cash flow requirements, and other
qualifying factors. Get an understanding of prepayment penalties, grace periods, and if/when
the lender can demand full repayment of the loan’s principal.
Why am I asked for my personal information?
The personally identifiable information you share will be used to connect you with prospective
SBA lenders. Registering and providing responses to the questionnaire is no guarantee that
SBA-approved lenders will find you eligible for their programs.
Which lenders participate?
More than 800 lenders participate in Lender Match throughout all 50 states and U.S.
territories. While all lenders who use Lender Match offer SBA-approved loans, many also
offer conventional loans.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
